titleStmt.title: Letter from James R. Martin to Robert Chalmers, 9 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James R. Martin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from High Sheriff of County Galway, James R. Martin, to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858–1938), the under-secretary of Ireland. Martin writes about the Rising in county Galway, claiming that the requests for compensation are false claims. He reports that Sinn Fein sympathisers willingly assisted the rebels and are now seeking to be refunded by the crown.The high sheriff of County Galway was the sovereign's judicial representative in County Galway. Sir Robert Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under-secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/7940/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Spoke to NJ
1. Ack.
11. Send extract
[ ] to CDB
111.See file he claims for compensation Rockmore, Athenry Co. Galway 9 May 1916 The Under Secretary The Castle Dublin Dear Sir I would like to be allowed to express an
opinion in the question of compensation for
injuries sustained in this county & the
recent Sinn Fein rising. Many claims
will be, & I think have been put in by people
who were thoroughly in sympathy with
the rebels & now make exorbitant claims
for articles that they gave them of their
own free will. Speaking broadly all
the people in this locality were in sympathy
with the rebels Also people who were not in sympathy
with them & have sustained injuries are
putting in exorbitant claims. I take from
the 'Galway Express' of the 6th with three
claims one for Â£160 one for Â£288.8.0
& one for Â£50 & my belief is that these
amounts are enormously in excess of the
value of the damage sustained. If I may be allowed to further express an
opinion as to this county, for which I am
responsible, I would like to say that the
leaders of this rising in Co. Galway several of whom have
not as yet been apprehended, are all men
well known to the police, and have for
years kept this County in a state of disturbance
& have been at the bottom of all outrages,
& are the recipients of German money.
It is their constant endeavour to keep the
County disturbed as if it becomes peaceful
their occupation & means of living are gone to
great extent, owning to them for many years
No one in this County has been able to embark
on any kind of business without first finding
an answer to the question; 'If this business
is successful shall I be allowed to carry it on'. I need hardly point out the advantage of
putting such disturbers of the public peace
in a position in which they will be no longer
harmful. I may perhaps also draw your attention
to another point some of the men who have
turned out as rebels, some of which are not
yet apprehended, have received from the
conjested district board excellent farms in
the vicinity of Athenry, & no doubt elsewhere,
& grants of money to build houses. Some
have received these things quite recently. It
is obviously not right that these men should
be allowed to retain these farms or pass
them on to their friends & relations. Yours faithfully James R. Martin
High Sheriff for the County Galway
